lesson.menu_desc: Agreeing & Disagreeing Gracefully, Hedging with Modals — 30 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 8 — Expressing Opinions and Agreeing or Disagreeing Gracefully
lesson.title_tag: Professor View — Vanessa Giuriati | Aula 8 — Expressing Opinions

listenings.text: Personally, I think remote work is one of the best changes of the last few years. From my perspective, it gives people more freedom and more time for their families. I know not everyone agrees, and I understand the other side. Some people feel lonely at home, and that is a fair point. But in my opinion, the benefits are bigger than the problems. Generally speaking, when people have more control over their day, they work better. Of course, it might not suit every job. A good solution could be a mix: some days at home, some days at the office. That, I believe, is the future. But honestly, I could be wrong. It is just my point of view.

People often ask me a hard question: is it easier to learn a language as a child or as an adult? Honestly, I think both have advantages. Children learn pronunciation more naturally, that is true. But I would argue that adults have something powerful too. We understand grammar, we have discipline, and we know why we are learning. From my perspective, the real difference is not age. It is motivation. An adult who really wants it can make amazing progress. I see the other side, of course. Time is harder to find when you are busy. But in my opinion, it is never too late to start. That is what I truly believe.

extra_audio.text: First, you give your opinion. Then, the other person agrees or disagrees. After that, you consider their point of view. If you disagree, you say it politely. Finally, you find common ground.
